what_it_does: Acceptance test that verifies a node marked VOLATILE is not frozen by the memo gate: it re-runs when its external filesystem source changes, while pure nodes still memo-cache on unchanged re-runs.
observations: Sets up a temporary FsStore, NodeRegistry, and Suite, then discovers nodes from the repo nodes directory.
Creates a throwaway source directory, writes an initial AGENTS.md, and runs a codebase node against it.
Mutates the external source by adding NEW.md between runs and asserts the codebase node re-runs and picks up the new content.
Builds a pure constant -> uppercase pipeline and asserts the uppercase node is memo-skipped on a second unchanged run.
Uses a global PASS counter inside a local check(label, cond) helper and a try/finally block to clean up the temp directory.
standouts: Directly tests the memo gate's VOLATILE opt-out by mutating real filesystem state between runs.
Asserts both the negative case (volatile not skipped) and the positive case (pure node still skipped).
Described in the module docstring as a red-team F1 fix.
conventions: Module-level test script with inline helper instead of a test framework.
Uses tempfile and shutil.rmtree with ignore_errors for cleanup.
Imports runtime and store modules and discovers nodes from a directory path.
concerns: check() mutates a global PASS counter and mixes assertion side effects with counting.
File handles from open(...) are not explicitly closed.
No parameterized or framework-based assertions; a single assertion failure aborts the whole script.
Hardcoded node/group IDs and inline config make the test brittle to node schema changes.
notes: A filesystem-mutation acceptance test that guards the VOLATILE memo-bypass behavior and confirms pure pipelines still cache.
questions: What exactly is the 'red-team F1 fix' and which failure mode it refers to?
How does Suite.run return the ran/skipped structure, and where is the VOLATILE flag enforced in the memo gate?
What are the exact semantics and schema of the constant and uppercase nodes used for the pure pipeline?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches what the file does: it tests that VOLATILE nodes re-run on external changes while pure nodes still memo-cache.
intention_signals: Prevent stale outputs from nodes that read changing external state by ensuring VOLATILE bypasses memoization, without breaking memoization for pure nodes.
novelty: It is a targeted acceptance test that exercises memoization behavior by mutating the filesystem between two runs of the same node.
contribution: A concrete, runnable acceptance test that documents and protects the invariant that VOLATILE nodes re-run on external changes while pure nodes remain memo-cacheable.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test in company/tests/volatile_acceptance.py that mutates a filesystem source between runs to verify a VOLATILE codebase node re-runs and a pure constant-to-uppercase pipeline still memo-caches.
intention_for_embedding: To guard the memo gate behavior where nodes reading external state must opt out of caching while pure nodes continue to benefit from memoization.

symbols.does: A local helper that asserts a condition, increments a global pass counter, and prints an ok message.
symbols.description: The check function is used throughout the script as a lightweight assertion helper. It takes a label and a condition, asserts the condition with a prefixed failure message, increments the module-level PASS counter, and prints a success line. It effectively replaces a test framework's assertion for this standalone acceptance script.
